topic: arithmetic sequences and patterns in number tables
latex:
The lattice shown is continued for 100 rows. What will be the third number in the $100^{\text{th}}$ row?
\[\begin{array}{r|ccccccc}
\text{Row } 1 & 1   & 2 & 3 & 4 & 5 & 6 & 7  \\
\text{Row } 2 & 8   & 9 & 10 & 11 & 12 & 13 & 14  \\
\text{Row } 3 & 15 & 16 & 17 & 18 & 19 & 20 & 21  \\
\text{Row } 4 & 22 & 23 & 24 &25  & 26 & 27 & 28  \\
\end{array}\]

}

\textbf{Answer:} Looking at the third number in each row, we get the sequence:  $3,10,17,24,\ldots$ This is an arithmetic sequence with common difference 7.

The third number in row 100 will the 100th number in this list. So, we begin at 3 and take 99 steps of 7 to reach the desired number: $3+99(7)=\boxed{696}$.
